California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Martha M. Norton 
26 El Caminito Del Norte 
Monterey, CA 
93940 
Dear Ms. Norton: 
October 10, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-532 
You have requested written advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act).l 
Since your question is general in nature and does not involve a 
specific decision, we are treating your request as one for 
informal assistance pu~suant to Regulation 18329(c).2 
QUESTION 
As a candidate for school board, do you face conflict-of-
interest problems because your husband is working part-time for 
the district as an independerit contractor under an early retire-
ment consultant contract? 
CONCLUSION 
The fact that your husband is under contract to the school 
district will not prevent you from participating in decisions af-
fecting the district. 
However, you will have to disqualify 
yourself from certain decisions relating to your husband's 
contract. 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
2 
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the 
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Government Code section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 
18329 (c) (3) .) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box R07 • 
S::ICT::ITrlpntr. l'A Qt:;.Q{'\.L{'\.Q('I'7 
• 
(OU;') 4-'-' C:':::':::A 


Our File No. 1-89-532 
Page 2 
FACTS 
You are a candidate for a seat on the Monterey Peninsula Uni-
fied School District Board. 
Your husband is a retired mathematics 
teacher. 
He is currently working part-time for the district as an 
early retirement consultant and is classified as an independent 
contiactor. 
The nature of the work performed is sUbstitute teach-
ing. 
He is working under a contract which was accepted in the 
fall of 1987. 
The conditions of the contract are not subject to 
change. 
You are concerned that his status as an independent contrac-
tor for the district may create a conflict of interest which might 
preclude you from continuing as a candidate or from serving on the 
school board. 
ANALYSIS 
If you are elected to the school board, you will be a public 
official who is subject to the conflict-of-interest provisions of 
the Act. 
(Section 82048; Regulation 18700, copy enclosed.) 
The 
Act prohibits public officials from mRking; participating in mak-
ing, or in any way attempting to use their official position to 
influence a governmental decision in which they know or have 
reason to know they have a financial interest. 
(Section 87100.) 
Whether an official has a financial interest in a decision is 
governed by section 87103, which provides as follows: 
An official has a financial interest in a 
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it 
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will 
have a material financial effect, distinguishable 
from its effect on the public generally, on the 
official or a member of his or her immediate family 
or on: 
* * 
* 
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and 
other than loans by a commercial lending institu-
tion in the regular course of business on terms 
available to the public without regard to official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars 
($250) or more in value provided to, received by or 
promised to the public official within 12 months 
prior to the time when the decision is made. 
section 87103(c). 


Our File No. I-89-532 
Page 3 
Under the Act, you are not prohibited from serving as a 
member of the school board while your husband is a retirement 
consultant for the district. 
However; there may be circumstances 
in which you must disqualify yourself from participating in 
particular governmental decisions. 
For the purposes of this analysis, we will assume that your 
husband's earnings from the school district are community 
property. 
Under the Act, "income" includes any community property 
interest in the income of a spouse. 
(Section 82030(a).) 
However, 
the Act excludes salary and per diem from a governmental agency 
from the definition of "income." 
(Section 82030(b) (2).) 
A school 
district is a governmental agency under the Act. 
(Section 82041.) 
While your husband is technically classified as an independent 
contractor, the fees that he receives under the contract are in 
the nature of compensation for services. 
We have previously found 
such compensation to be in the nature of salary and excludable 
from income under section 83030(b) (2). 
(Ritchie Advice Letter, 
No. A-79-045; Lyon Advice Letter, 
No. A-88-391, copies enclosed.) 
Therefore, your husband's posi-
tion as a retirement consultant for the school district will 
generally nnt crea~e a conflict of interest situation for 
Regulation 18702.1 (a) (4) (copy enclosed) prohibits a public 
official from participating in any decision which would 
foreseeably increase or decrease the personal expenses, income, 
assets or liabilities of the official or his or her immediate fam-
ily by at least $250. 
However, the regulation further provides: 
(c) 
Notwithstanding subsection (a) an 
official does not have to disqualify himself 
or herself from a governmental decision if: 
(1) 
The decision only affects the 
salary, per diem, or reimbursement for 
expenses the official or his or her 
spouse receives from a state or local 
government agency. 
This subsection does 
not apply to decisions to hire, fire, 
promote, demote, or discipline an 
official's spouse, or to set a salary for 
an official's spouse which is different 
from salaries paid to other employees of 
the spouse's agency in the same job 
classification or position; 
Regulation 18702.1 (c) (2) 
Therefore, you will only have to disqualify yourself from 
decisions before the district which affect your husband uniquely 


Our File No. I-89-532 
Page 4 
in a manner similar to the examples set forth in the regulation. 
(See, Plisky Advice Letter, No. A-86-149, copy enclosed.) 
If you have any questions regarding this matter, you may 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KEDjMWEjaa 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
By: 
Margaret W. Ellison 
counsel, Legal Division 


FPPC 
SEP " 9 01 ~H '89 
Margaret Ellison 
Counsel, Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Division 
428 J Street 
Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Margaret: 
Martha M. Norton 
26 El Caminito Del Norte 
Monterey, CA 
93940 
September 7,1989 
As per our telephone conversation earlier today, I am writing 
to you so that I may obtain in writing the conditions and/or 
restrictions that might apply if I am successful as a candidate 
for the Monterey Peninsula Unified School District Board. 
I am a retired physics teacher and have completed my five 
years as an early retirement consultant. 
My husband, Joe, a 
mathematics teacher of 34 years, retired in June of 1989. 
He is currently working for 30 days as allowed by contract 
in the early retirement plan of consultatnt as independent 
contractor. 
He is working under a contract which was 
accepted in the fall of 1987. 
The conditions of that contract 
are not subject to change. 
My question 
is are there any conditions or restrictions 
of consequence to either my candidacy or actions as a board 
member which JbUld be considered either for withdrawal from 
the race, or for voting legally as a board member, if elected? 
Thank you for any consideration given this request. 
Sincerely, 
Martha M. Norton 
Enclosures: 
Indicate activities to date. 


MARTHA NORTON FOR SCHOOL BOARD 
2161 Trapani Circle 
Monterey, California 93940 
August 28, 1989 
Dear Friends: 
We are writing to ask for your active support for Martha Norton as she seeks election 
to the Governing Board of the Monterey Peninsula Unified School District. We are 
delighted that Martha is running for this position, and we hope you will join us in making 
her campaign successful. 
Recent state and national elections have brought public school education to the 
forefront as CAMPAIGN ISSUES, but tht: PERFORMANCE of uur dt:ded It:adt:rs AFTER the 
election still leaves much to be desired. We believe the same can be said of many of our 
locally elected school officials. It is so easy and comfortable to maintain the status quo. 
Martha is eminently qualified for this job. After working as a chemist in private 
industry, Martha became a physics teacher in the Monterey public schools. During her 
teaching career, Martha worked through professional associations and with parents, 
students, teachers, and school administrators to bring about better learning opportunities for 
students. Even after retirement, she devoted much of her time, energy and talent to helping 
students prepare Science Fair projects. She maintains a continuing interest in public school 
education. 
But Martha's dedication to our community does not end with education. It has been 
our privilege to work with Martha in a variety of community endeavors, including safe 
disposal of hazardous waste, bringing a recycling program to Monterey, and successful 
political and initiative campaigns. Whenever there is a job to be done, we have found 
Martha willing to do more than her part. We have found her to be extremely effective in 
working with others to bring about meaningful change. We know she wiII continue to do 
so as a school boa~d member. 
As such, she will bring her own special qualifications to bear on budget analysis and 
spending priorities and represent us and our ideas in the forum where it can do the most 
good--on the Monterey Peninsula Unified School District Board of Education. 
Please help us help Martha by responding with your endorsement and financial 
contribution. Support and vote for MARTHA NORTON, who has done so much for us and 
our community. 
Ruth Menmuir 
Co-Chair 
Lorita Fisher 
Co-Chair

Harold Ra~:d'" 
Treasurer

CANDIDATE'S STATEMENT 
GOVERNING BOARD MEMBER 
MONTEREY PENN UNIF. TRUST. 2 
MARTHA NORTON 
RETIRED 
Age: 
During the past decade the Monterey Peninsula Unified Schoo+l----
District (MPUSD) has become a much respected school district. 
It has received the National Library Media Program Award designating 
it as the outstanding School District Library Program in the 
United States. 
It has been selected as a State Hodel Technology 
site placing it in the forefront of educational institutions 
researching the effects of technology on learning. 
It has 
attracted superior employees at every level. 
However. the District 
is not without its problems. 
It faces decisions regarding the 
costly construction of a new high school or renovation of our 
existing ones, and the hiring of key top level management personnel. 
It faces difficulties in implementing Proposition 98 as approved 
by our voters, who believed their vote assured t~dget security 
with accountability and lower class size. 
It recognizes that many 
parents of middle and high school students send their children to 
local private schools for the advantages of lower class size as well 
as a more rigorous academic climate .• 
~ 
I feel that my professional and community service background 
provides me with the experience necessary to contribute to solving 
the issues facing the district. 
My professional background 
includes experience as a Chemical Research Librarian for Shell 
Development and Research Company, a Nuclear Engineer for Lockheed 
Aircraft-Nuclear Division and twenty-five years as a Physics 
teacher. 
My recent community service includes membership on the 
Monterey Cbunty Hazardous Waste Management Advisory Committee, 
AMBAG'S Technical Advisory Committee for the Salinas River Water 
Quality Project, and the City of Monterey's Curbside Recycling 
Committee (as a representative for the League of Komen Voters). 
Experience on these comrr:ittees has tauaht me the value of 
listening carefully to problems presented, securlng 411 significant 
data available. setting r~a:istic goals. and wor~ing cooperatively

to you so that I may obtain in writing the conditions and/or

for the Monterey Peninsula Unified School District Board. 
I am a retired physics teacher and have completed my five 
years as an early retirement consultant. 
My husband, Joe, a 
mathematics teacher of 34 years, retired in June of 1989. 
He is currently working for 30 days as allowed by contract 
in the early retirement plan of consultatnt as independent 
contractor. 
He is working under a contract which was 
accepted in the fall of 1987. 
The conditions of that contract 
are not subject to change. 
My question 
is are there any conditions or restrictions 
of consequence to either my candidacy or actions as a board

MARTHA NORTON FOR SCHOOL BOARD 
2161 Trapani Circle 
Monterey, California 93940 
August 28, 1989 
Dear Friends: 
We are writing to ask for your active support for Martha Norton as she seeks election 
to the Governing Board of the Monterey Peninsula Unified School District. We are 
delighted that Martha is running for this position, and we hope you will join us in making 
her campaign successful. 
Recent state and national elections have brought public school education to the 
forc-front as CAMPAIGN ISSUES, but the PERFORMANCE uf uur deded leaders AFTER the 
election still leaves much to be desired. We believe the same can be said of many of our 
locally elected school officials. It is so easy and comfortable to maintain the status quo. 
Martha is eminently qualified for this job. After working as a chemist in private 
industry, Martha became a physics teacher in the Monterey public schools. During her 
teaching career, Martha worked through professional associations and with parents, 
students, teachers, and school administrators to bring about better learning opportunities for 
students. Even after retirement, she devoted much of her time, energy and talent to helping 
students prepare Science Fair projects. She maintains a continuing interest in public school 
education. 
But Martha's dedication to our community does not end with education. It has been 
our privilege to work with Martha in a variety of community endeavors, including safe 
disposal of hazardous waste, bringing a recycling program to Monterey, and successful 
political and initiative campaigns. Whenever there is a job to be done, we have found 
Martha willing to do more than her part. We have found her to be extremely effective in 
working with others to bring about meaningful change. We know she will continue to do 
so as a school board member.

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Martha M. Norton 
26 El Caminito Del Norte 
Monterey, CA 
93940 
Dear Ms. Norton: 
September 13, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-532 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on September 11, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Margaret Ellison an attorney in the Legal 
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel
